Flowstate
WhyVisionCanvasArtifactsInputsBegin
Open canvas

Flowstate

Your thoughts deserve a map, not a scroll

When you research, plan, or figure something out, your mind branches. Flowstate gives you a canvas where every question stays where you put it — and nothing gets buried above the fold.

Press Enter to open the canvas with your question

See why spatial thinking helps you

Why this exists

Linear chat was never built for how you think

You jump between threads, lose the earlier branch, and scroll back hunting for context. On a canvas, your parallel lines stay visible — and you always know where you are.

Toggle to feel the difference in your own session

One thread — earlier branches disappear as you scroll
Where should I spend my long weekend?
Compare Lisbon neighborhoods
Wait — what about Porto?
Back to the Lisbon thread…
Summarize everything so far

What changes for you

Hold more in your head, with less effort

You can explore a side question without abandoning the main one. Dead ends stay on the map. When two threads connect, you see it — because the shape of your session is right in front of you.

01

You branch without guilt

Follow a tangent — compare Porto, check train times, read about food — without losing the thread you started from.

02

You see where you've been

Your session has a shape. Zoom out and notice which paths went cold, which ones are still alive.

03

You pick up where you left off

Come back tomorrow. Your cards are still in the same place. Your spatial memory does the rest.

Flowstate is not about getting answers faster. It is about giving you room for the full shape of a question — including the dead ends, the detours, and the moment two threads unexpectedly meet.

The canvas

Same cards, same curves — the real product

Pan, zoom, and branch exactly like you would in Flowstate. Press Q to drop a question. Pull a side plug to split a thread. Every card keeps the full context of what came before.

Step 1

Drop a question

Press Q anywhere on the canvas — your cursor is the placement.

Step 2

Branch when you need to

Pull a side plug on any card to explore a parallel line without starting over.

Step 3

Navigate your terrain

Pan and zoom. Every card keeps the full context of its ancestors.

What you can make

Tables, maps, charts — whatever your question needs

These are the same artifact components from the canvas. When we improve them in the product, you see it here too.

Tables

Organize metrics, comparisons, and structured lists in a sortable grid. Status and category labels appear as chips inside cells.

financial planningproduct comparisonresearch summariesmetrics
Aa
CityNightsLodgingStatus
Lisbon
4
€420
BookedConfirmed
Porto
3
€310
ShortlistCompare
Madrid
2
€240
DraftPending

Charts — bar

Compare categories with bar charts. Switch type and style via the toolbar — ECharts or visx, plain background.

budgetingspendingpersonal financecomparison

Charts — area

Filled trends with visx AreaClosed curves by default. Try stream graph when you have multiple series.

fitnessrunningtrendshealth

Charts — line

Time-series line graphs with smooth visx curves. Toggle ECharts straight or smooth styles.

sleepwellnesshabitstracking

Charts — pie

Share-of-whole breakdowns for personal time, budget slices, or habit splits.

weekendtime managementlifestylebalance

Charts — gauge

Track progress toward a personal goal with a plain gauge or progress bar.

savingsgoalsfinanceprogress

Code

Explore implementations across one or more files with syntax highlighting. Supports HTML, CSS, Python, TypeScript, and any text format.

API designdebuggingimplementation walkthroughsprototyping
Aa
const windowMs = 60_000;
const maxRequests = 100;

export function checkLimit(count: number): boolean {
  return count <= maxRequests;
}

3D preview

Preview 3D models directly on the canvas. Point to a model URL and rotate or inspect the asset in context.

product designarchitecturegame assetsvisualization
Aa
Loading model…

Maps

Pin a geocodable place — city, state, or country — for travel and location context. Saved pins persist when editing.

travelgeographytrip planninglocation research

Street view

Open Google Street View at a specific named venue — station, hotel, landmark, or address — for a street-level look.

travelreal estatevenue scoutingnavigation

To-do lists

Track actionable items with checkboxes, optional due dates, and priority levels. Use instead of prose lists for tasks.

project planningpacking listssprint taskserrands

Calendars

View a month at a glance with highlighted dates and all-day events. Ideal for scheduling, not chronology over years.

schedulingdeadlinestrip datesevent planning
Aa

June 2026

Su
Mo
Tu
We
Th
Fr
Sa

Timelines

Map events across time on a horizontal axis. Use for history, roadmaps, and milestones — not month-grid scheduling.

historyroadmapsproduct milestonesbiographies
100%
Aa

Beta launch

May 2026

Custom UI

Build self-contained interactive components — timers, forms, dashboards, calculators. The artifact is the deliverable.

dashboardscalculatorsformswidgets
Aa

What you can bring

Paste a link, drop a file, attach a skill

Bring your references into a question — images, PDFs, YouTube links, GitHub repos, Google Docs, and more. The composer uses the same attachment UI as the app.

Websites

Paste a generic URL to spawn a preview card with title, domain, favicon, and optional preview image.

researchdocumentationreference linksbookmarks

nextjs.org

nextjs.org/

Images

Paste image URLs or search Wikimedia for real photographs of places, people, and things.

mood boardsreference photosvisual researchtravel inspiration
Aa

Videos

Paste a YouTube or video URL to create a grid of playable clips with thumbnails and titles.

tutorialstalkstravel vlogsdemos

Audio — short clip

Drop MP3, WAV, M4A, AAC, OGG, or WebM audio onto the canvas. Waveform width scales with duration.

voice memosinterviewspodcast clipsfield recordings
Voice memo0:00 / 1:00

Audio preview unavailable.

Audio — long clip

Longer clips spawn wider nodes — a 5-minute file is five times the waveform width of a 1-minute file.

meetingslecturesambientmusic sketches
Meeting recording0:00 / 5:00

Audio preview unavailable.

Repositories

Paste a GitHub URL to explore a repository with overview, file structure, media, preview, tech stack, and built-by widgets.

code explorationOSS evaluationonboardingdue diligence
⋮⋮Drag

vercel/next.js

Fetching repository…

Built by

Embeds

Paste URLs from Reddit, Twitter, Instagram, Facebook, Medium, Substack, or Figma for embedded previews.

social proofdesign refsthreadsarticles
Loading embed…

Free-form prompts

Open-ended questions and brainstorming spawn text cards and can trigger any artifact type the AI deems fit.

brainstormingQ&Aopen-ended inquiryexploration
What are good day-trip options from Lisbon?

Sintra and Cascais are the easiest wins — both are under an hour by train and pair well with a long weekend in the city.

Begin

Open a blank canvas and start where you are

No setup ritual. Drop your first question, branch when you need to, and come back tomorrow — your session keeps its shape.

Open canvas
Loading…

Pull a thread from the side plugs when a tangent is worth its own line — you do not have to restart the conversation.

Q

Press Q anywhere on the canvas to place a question exactly where your cursor is.

Scroll to zoom. Hold Space and drag to pan. Your map stays intact as you move through it.

Flowstate — your spatial thinking canvas
